exercises.title: Detector Performance Analysis
exercises.description: Implement a comprehensive detector model that calculates key performance metrics including signal-to-noise ratio, coincidence fidelity, and count rate limitations.
exercises.starterCode:

class SinglePhotonDetector:
    """
    Model for a single-photon detector with realistic parameters.
    """

                 timing_jitter_ps: float, dead_time_ns: float):

        Initialize detector model.
        
        Args:
            efficiency: Detection efficiency (0 to 1)
            dark_count_rate: Dark counts per second (Hz)
            timing_jitter_ps: Timing jitter in picoseconds
            dead_time_ns: Dead time in nanoseconds
        """
        self.eta = efficiency
        self.dark_rate = dark_count_rate
        self.jitter_ps = timing_jitter_ps
        self.dead_time_ns = dead_time_ns
    
    def max_count_rate(self) -> float:
        """
        Calculate maximum achievable count rate limited by dead time.
        
        Returns:
            Maximum count rate in Hz
        """
        # Your code here
        pass
    
    def actual_count_rate(self, incident_photon_rate: float) -> float:
        """
        Calculate actual count rate accounting for efficiency and dead time.
        
        Args:
            incident_photon_rate: Photons per second hitting detector
        
        Returns:
            Actual detected count rate in Hz
        """
        # Your code here
        # Hint: Use saturation model R_out = R_in / (1 + R_in * dead_time)
        pass
    
    def signal_to_noise(self, signal_rate: float, 
                        integration_time: float) -> float:
        """
        Calculate signal-to-noise ratio.
        
        Args:
            signal_rate: True photon rate in Hz
            integration_time: Measurement time in seconds
        
        Returns:
            SNR value
        """
        # Your code here
        pass
    
    def hom_visibility_limit(self, photon_coherence_time_ps: float) -> float:
        """
        Calculate HOM visibility limit due to timing jitter.
        
        Args:
            photon_coherence_time_ps: Photon coherence time in ps
        
        Returns:
            Maximum achievable HOM visibility

# Define typical detectors
spad = SinglePhotonDetector(efficiency=0.6, dark_count_rate=100,
                            timing_jitter_ps=100, dead_time_ns=50)
snspd = SinglePhotonDetector(efficiency=0.95, dark_count_rate=1,
                             timing_jitter_ps=20, dead_time_ns=10)

# Compare performance
print("Detector Comparison:")
print(f"{'Metric':<25} {'SPAD':>12} {'SNSPD':>12}")
print("-" * 50)
print(f"{'Max count rate (MHz)':<25} {spad.max_count_rate()/1e6:>12.2f} {snspd.max_count_rate()/1e6:>12.2f}")
print(f"{'SNR (1MHz signal, 1s)':<25} {spad.signal_to_noise(1e6, 1):>12.1f} {snspd.signal_to_noise(1e6, 1):>12.1f}")
print(f"{'HOM limit (100ps coh)':<25} {spad.hom_visibility_limit(100):>12.4f} {snspd.hom_visibility_limit(100):>12.4f}")
exercises.solution:
import numpy as np

class SinglePhotonDetector:
    """
    Model for a single-photon detector with realistic parameters.
    """
    
    def __init__(self, efficiency: float, dark_count_rate: float,
                 timing_jitter_ps: float, dead_time_ns: float):
        self.eta = efficiency
        self.dark_rate = dark_count_rate
        self.jitter_ps = timing_jitter_ps
        self.dead_time_ns = dead_time_ns
    
    def max_count_rate(self) -> float:
        """
        Maximum count rate limited by dead time.
        """
        dead_time_s = self.dead_time_ns * 1e-9
        return 1 / dead_time_s
    
    def actual_count_rate(self, incident_photon_rate: float) -> float:
        """
        Actual count rate with saturation.
        """
        dead_time_s = self.dead_time_ns * 1e-9
        detected_rate = incident_photon_rate * self.eta
        # Paralyzable detector model
        return detected_rate / (1 + detected_rate * dead_time_s)
    
    def signal_to_noise(self, signal_rate: float,
                        integration_time: float) -> float:
        """
        Signal-to-noise ratio.
        """
        detected_signal = signal_rate * self.eta
        total_rate = detected_signal + self.dark_rate
        
        signal_counts = detected_signal * integration_time
        noise_counts = np.sqrt(total_rate * integration_time)
        
        return signal_counts / noise_counts if noise_counts > 0 else 0
    
    def hom_visibility_limit(self, photon_coherence_time_ps: float) -> float:
        """
        HOM visibility limit from timing jitter.
        """
        # Combined jitter from two detectors (assuming identical)
        combined_jitter = np.sqrt(2) * self.jitter_ps
        
        # Visibility reduction factor
        visibility = np.exp(-(combined_jitter / photon_coherence_time_ps) ** 2)
        
        return visibility

# Test
spad = SinglePhotonDetector(efficiency=0.6, dark_count_rate=100,
                            timing_jitter_ps=100, dead_time_ns=50)
snspd = SinglePhotonDetector(efficiency=0.95, dark_count_rate=1,
                             timing_jitter_ps=20, dead_time_ns=10)

print("Detector Comparison:")
print(f"{'Metric':<25} {'SPAD':>12} {'SNSPD':>12}")
print("-" * 50)
print(f"{'Max count rate (MHz)':<25} {spad.max_count_rate()/1e6:>12.2f} {snspd.max_count_rate()/1e6:>12.2f}")
print(f"{'SNR (1MHz signal, 1s)':<25} {spad.signal_to_noise(1e6, 1):>12.1f} {snspd.signal_to_noise(1e6, 1):>12.1f}")
print(f"{'HOM limit (100ps coh)':<25} {spad.hom_visibility_limit(100):>12.4f} {snspd.hom_visibility_limit(100):>12.4f}")
print(f"{'HOM limit (1000ps coh)':<25} {spad.hom_visibility_limit(1000):>12.4f} {snspd.hom_visibility_limit(1000):>12.4f}")
hints: Maximum count rate is limited by 1/dead_time
Use the saturation model: R_out = R_in * eta / (1 + R_in * eta * dead_time)
HOM visibility is reduced by exp(-(jitter/coherence_time)^2)

exercises.title: Photon Number Resolution with Detector Arrays
exercises.description: Implement a model for photon-number-resolving detection using an array of single-photon detectors. Calculate the probability distribution of click patterns and the photon number reconstruction accuracy.
exercises.starterCode:

from scipy.special import comb
from itertools import product

class PNRDetectorArray:
    """
    Photon-number-resolving detection using spatial multiplexing.

        self.N = n_detectors
        self.eta = single_detector_efficiency
        self.p_dark = dark_count_prob
    
    def click_distribution(self, n_photons: int) -> np.ndarray:

        Calculate probability distribution of number of clicks.
        Assumes uniform distribution of photons across detectors.
        
        Args:
            n_photons: Number of incident photons
        
        Returns:

        Returns:

        """

    def estimate_photon_number(self, n_clicks: int, prior: np.ndarray) -> np.ndarray:

            prior: Prior probability distribution P(n photons)
        
        Returns:

        Fidelity = probability of correctly identifying photon number.

# Test the PNR detector
pnr = PNRDetectorArray(n_detectors=16, single_detector_efficiency=0.9,
                       dark_count_prob=0.001)

print("Click distribution for 3 photons:")
clicks = pnr.click_distribution(3)
for k, p in enumerate(clicks):
    if p > 0.001:
        print(f"  P({k} clicks) = {p:.4f}")

print("\nReconstruction matrix (partial):")
R = pnr.reconstruction_matrix(5)
print("P(k|n) for k=0..5, n=0..5:")
print(R[:6, :6].round(3))

print(f"\nOverall fidelity: {pnr.fidelity(10):.4f}")
exercises.solution:
import numpy as np
from scipy.special import comb
from itertools import product

class PNRDetectorArray:
    """
    Photon-number-resolving detection using spatial multiplexing.
    """
    
    def __init__(self, n_detectors: int, single_detector_efficiency: float,
                 dark_count_prob: float = 0.0):
        self.N = n_detectors
        self.eta = single_detector_efficiency
        self.p_dark = dark_count_prob
    
    def click_distribution(self, n_photons: int) -> np.ndarray:
        """
        Calculate probability distribution of number of clicks.
        """
        N = self.N
        n = n_photons
        eta = self.eta
        p_dark = self.p_dark
        
        probs = np.zeros(N + 1)
        
        if n == 0:
            # Only dark counts possible
            for k in range(N + 1):
                probs[k] = comb(N, k, exact=True) * (p_dark ** k) * ((1 - p_dark) ** (N - k))
        else:
            # Photons distributed across detectors
            for k in range(min(n, N) + 1):
                # Probability of k clicks from n photons (simplified model)
                # Assumes photons uniformly distributed
                if n <= N:
                    # Each photon in different detector
                    p_k = comb(n, k, exact=True) * (eta ** k) * ((1 - eta) ** (n - k))
                    # Adjust for actual distribution
                    probs[k] = p_k
                else:
                    # Multiple photons per detector possible
                    # Use approximation
                    p_hit = 1 - (1 - 1/N) ** n  # Prob detector hit by at least one
                    p_click = p_hit * eta
                    probs[k] = comb(N, k, exact=True) * (p_click ** k) * ((1 - p_click) ** (N - k))
        
        # Normalize
        probs = probs / np.sum(probs) if np.sum(probs) > 0 else probs
        return probs
    
    def reconstruction_matrix(self, max_photons: int) -> np.ndarray:
        """
        Calculate forward matrix P(k clicks | n photons).
        """
        R = np.zeros((self.N + 1, max_photons + 1))
        for n in range(max_photons + 1):
            R[:, n] = self.click_distribution(n)
        return R
    
    def estimate_photon_number(self, n_clicks: int, prior: np.ndarray) -> np.ndarray:
        """
        Bayesian inference for photon number.
        """
        max_n = len(prior) - 1
        R = self.reconstruction_matrix(max_n)
        
        # P(k|n) * P(n)
        likelihood_times_prior = R[n_clicks, :] * prior
        
        # Normalize to get posterior
        posterior = likelihood_times_prior / np.sum(likelihood_times_prior)
        return posterior
    
    def fidelity(self, max_photons: int) -> float:
        """
        Average photon number discrimination fidelity.
        """
        R = self.reconstruction_matrix(max_photons)
        
        # For each n, find most likely k, and P(correct) = P(n|k) * P(k|n)
        total_fidelity = 0
        for n in range(max_photons + 1):
            # Simple metric: probability that k=n
            if n <= self.N:
                total_fidelity += R[n, n]
        
        return total_fidelity / (max_photons + 1)

# Test
pnr = PNRDetectorArray(n_detectors=16, single_detector_efficiency=0.9,
                       dark_count_prob=0.001)

print("Click distribution for 3 photons:")
clicks = pnr.click_distribution(3)
for k, p in enumerate(clicks):
    if p > 0.001:
        print(f"  P({k} clicks) = {p:.4f}")

print("\nReconstruction matrix (partial):")
R = pnr.reconstruction_matrix(5)
print("P(k|n) for k=0..5, n=0..5:")
print(R[:6, :6].round(3))

print(f"\nOverall fidelity: {pnr.fidelity(10):.4f}")

# Bayesian estimation example
print("\nBayesian estimation with uniform prior:")
prior = np.ones(6) / 6
posterior = pnr.estimate_photon_number(3, prior)
print(f"Observed 3 clicks -> Posterior: {posterior.round(3)}")
hints: For n <= N photons, each can hit a different detector
For n > N, multiple photons per detector causes undercounting
Use Bayes' theorem for reconstruction: P(n|k) proportional to P(k|n)*P(n)

testCases.expected: Likely to see 8 or fewer clicks due to multiplexing

exercises.title: Coincidence Detection Analysis
exercises.description: Implement analysis of coincidence detection for heralded single-photon experiments. Calculate accidental coincidence rates and the Coincidence-to-Accidental Ratio (CAR).
exercises.starterCode:
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

class CoincidenceAnalyzer:
    """
    Analyze coincidence detection in heralded photon experiments.
    """
    
    def __init__(self, herald_rate: float, signal_rate: float,
                 coincidence_window: float, herald_dark_rate: float = 0,
                 signal_dark_rate: float = 0):
        """
        Initialize analyzer.
        
        Args:
            herald_rate: True herald detection rate (Hz)
            signal_rate: True signal detection rate (Hz)
            coincidence_window: Coincidence time window (seconds)
            herald_dark_rate: Herald detector dark count rate (Hz)
            signal_dark_rate: Signal detector dark count rate (Hz)
        """
        self.R_h = herald_rate
        self.R_s = signal_rate
        self.tau = coincidence_window
        self.D_h = herald_dark_rate
        self.D_s = signal_dark_rate
    
    def true_coincidence_rate(self) -> float:
        """
        Calculate rate of true coincidences.
        
        Returns:
            True coincidence rate in Hz
        """
        # Your code here
        pass
    
    def accidental_coincidence_rate(self) -> float:
        """
        Calculate rate of accidental coincidences.
        Accidentals = R_total_h * R_total_s * tau
        
        Returns:
            Accidental coincidence rate in Hz
        """
        # Your code here
        pass
    
    def car(self) -> float:
        """
        Calculate Coincidence-to-Accidental Ratio.
        CAR = true_coincidences / accidental_coincidences
        
        Returns:
            CAR value
        """
        # Your code here
        pass
    
    def g2_heralded(self) -> float:
        """
        Calculate g^(2)(0) for heralded signal photons.
        
        Returns:
            g^(2)(0) value
        """
        # Your code here
        # Hint: Related to CAR and multi-pair probability
        pass
    
    def optimize_window(self, window_range: np.ndarray) -> tuple:
        """
        Find optimal coincidence window that maximizes CAR.
        
        Args:
            window_range: Array of window values to test (seconds)
        
        Returns:
            (optimal_window, max_car)
        """
        # Your code here
        pass

# Test with realistic parameters
analyzer = CoincidenceAnalyzer(
    herald_rate=1e6,      # 1 MHz true heralds
    signal_rate=0.8e6,    # 0.8 MHz signals (80% collection efficiency)
    coincidence_window=1e-9,  # 1 ns window
    herald_dark_rate=100,
    signal_dark_rate=100
)

print(f"True coincidence rate: {analyzer.true_coincidence_rate()/1e3:.2f} kHz")
print(f"Accidental coincidence rate: {analyzer.accidental_coincidence_rate():.2f} Hz")
print(f"CAR: {analyzer.car():.1f}")
print(f"g^(2)(0) heralded: {analyzer.g2_heralded():.4f}")

# Find optimal window
windows = np.logspace(-10, -7, 50)  # 100 ps to 100 ns
opt_window, max_car = analyzer.optimize_window(windows)
print(f"\nOptimal window: {opt_window*1e9:.2f} ns, CAR: {max_car:.1f}")
exercises.solution:
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

class CoincidenceAnalyzer:
    """
    Analyze coincidence detection in heralded photon experiments.
    """
    
    def __init__(self, herald_rate: float, signal_rate: float,
                 coincidence_window: float, herald_dark_rate: float = 0,
                 signal_dark_rate: float = 0):
        self.R_h = herald_rate
        self.R_s = signal_rate
        self.tau = coincidence_window
        self.D_h = herald_dark_rate
        self.D_s = signal_dark_rate
    
    def true_coincidence_rate(self) -> float:
        """
        Rate of true coincidences (assuming perfect correlation).
        """
        # True coincidences occur when both herald and signal are real
        # Rate limited by the lower of the two
        return min(self.R_h, self.R_s)
    
    def accidental_coincidence_rate(self) -> float:
        """
        Rate of accidental coincidences.
        """
        total_herald = self.R_h + self.D_h
        total_signal = self.R_s + self.D_s
        return total_herald * total_signal * self.tau
    
    def car(self) -> float:
        """
        Coincidence-to-Accidental Ratio.
        """
        true = self.true_coincidence_rate()
        accidental = self.accidental_coincidence_rate()
        return true / accidental if accidental > 0 else float('inf')
    
    def g2_heralded(self) -> float:
        """
        g^(2)(0) for heralded signal.
        For SPDC: g2 ~ 2/CAR in low-gain limit
        """
        car = self.car()
        return 2 / car if car > 0 else 2.0
    
    def optimize_window(self, window_range: np.ndarray) -> tuple:
        """
        Find optimal window maximizing CAR.
        Note: Shorter window reduces accidentals but may miss true coincidences.
        """
        best_car = 0
        best_window = window_range[0]
        
        original_tau = self.tau
        for tau in window_range:
            self.tau = tau
            current_car = self.car()
            if current_car > best_car:
                best_car = current_car
                best_window = tau
        
        self.tau = original_tau
        return (best_window, best_car)

# Test
analyzer = CoincidenceAnalyzer(
    herald_rate=1e6,
    signal_rate=0.8e6,
    coincidence_window=1e-9,
    herald_dark_rate=100,
    signal_dark_rate=100
)

print(f"True coincidence rate: {analyzer.true_coincidence_rate()/1e3:.2f} kHz")
print(f"Accidental coincidence rate: {analyzer.accidental_coincidence_rate():.2f} Hz")
print(f"CAR: {analyzer.car():.1f}")
print(f"g^(2)(0) heralded: {analyzer.g2_heralded():.4f}")

windows = np.logspace(-10, -7, 50)
opt_window, max_car = analyzer.optimize_window(windows)
print(f"\nOptimal window: {opt_window*1e9:.2f} ns, CAR: {max_car:.1f}")

# Plot CAR vs window
car_values = []
original_tau = analyzer.tau
for tau in windows:
    analyzer.tau = tau
    car_values.append(analyzer.car())
analyzer.tau = original_tau

plt.figure(figsize=(10, 5))
plt.loglog(windows * 1e9, car_values, 'b-', linewidth=2)
plt.xlabel('Coincidence Window (ns)')
plt.ylabel('CAR')
plt.title('Coincidence-to-Accidental Ratio vs Window Size')
plt.grid(True)
plt.axvline(x=opt_window*1e9, color='r', linestyle='--', label=f'Optimal: {opt_window*1e9:.2f} ns')
plt.legend()
plt.show()
hints: True coincidences depend on actual photon pair correlations
CAR = True/Accidental is a key quality metric for heralded sources

testCases.input: 1 MHz rates, 1 ns window, low dark counts

testCases.expected: Lower CAR due to more accidentals
